title: Disable Copilot in VS Code
author: Holly
text:
I want to completely disable Copilot in VS Code.
There are some old questions about this, but VS Code has changed enough to make them obsolete:
- Copilot is no longer an extension;
- Ctrl+Shift+P no longer has a "hide copilot" or "disable copilot" entry; and
settings.json
no longer thinksgithub.copilot.enable
is a thing.
I'm on Windows 11 (24H2) with VS Code 1.102.3.
So far I've got the latest recommended:
"chat.agent.enabled": false,
"chat.commandCenter.enabled": false,
"chat.notifyWindowOnConfirmation": false,
but when I use the {}
button next to the file type (e.g. Plain Text
) I still get the Copilot icon with the text "No inline suggestion available", so it seems I haven't got rid of it completely.
